title: Model salience mapping and stakeholder cost synthesis
summary: Produce a prioritized mapping that links model capability salience to buyer personas and quantifies switching / fallback costs for procurement and SRE stakeholders.
text:
Model salience mapping and stakeholder cost synthesis

Produce a prioritized mapping that links model capability salience to buyer personas and quantifies switching / fallback costs for procurement and SRE stakeholders.
We have a large backlog of model signals but limited signal-to-decision workflows for prioritizing which new or emergent models matter to our customers. Sales and SRE teams ask three recurring questions: which model features change buyer willingness to switch, what are the operational costs of supporting fallbacks, and which small changes (latency/quantization) materially change adoption. This ticket creates a repeatable mapping and cost synthesis to inform procurement triggers and the model watchlist.
Create a model salience heatmap linking feature deltas (latency, accuracy on vertical prompts, token cost, API parity) to buyer personas
Estimate switching and fallback costs (integration, QA, observability, SLA delta) per model cohort
Produce a decision rubric for field teams to prioritize scouting and provisioning actions
Deliver artifacts: heatmap, one-page synthesis, prioritized model list, and recommended telemetry to capture going forward
Focus on models likely to influence mid-market and enterprise adoption in Q2 2025: general-purpose open models, quantized variants, and two promising narrow-domain entrants identified by the scouting team. Exclude multimodal/voice models for now. Both technical and commercial levers considered.
Synthesize existing signals from model-scouting dataset and usage telemetry (top 500 customer prompts, latency sensitivity buckets)
Run 4 synthetic workloads (customer representative prompts across verticals: finserv, docs, search, chat) against 6 candidate models to capture delta metrics
Conduct 6 structured interviews with field sellers and 4 with SRE/ops to capture qualitative cost drivers
Calculate conservative cost estimates for integration, monitoring, and SLAs to produce a per-model switching cost range
Produce heatmap and rubric; iterate with CRO and Head of SRE
Model salience heatmap (feature x persona) - slide deck
Stakeholder cost synthesis - spreadsheet and one-pager
Prioritized model list with recommended action (monitor, probe, onboard) - CSV
Decision rubric for procurement and field - markdown doc
Telemetry spec to capture buyer signal and fallback triggers - RFC draft
Field teams can classify 80% of emergent model signals with actionable priority within 48 hours
Top 10 prioritized models include >=2 with trial-worthy performance based on synthetic workloads
Procurement triggers defined and accepted by sales (sign-off from CRO)
Heatmap and one-pager reviewed and approved by CRO and Head of SRE
Synthetic workload results reproducible in CI and linked in the deliverable spreadsheet
Decision rubric includes clear thresholds for monitor/probe/onboard and is integrated into the scouting workflow
Telemetry RFC creates at least 3 new signals ingested into model-scouting over next sprint
Risk: Synthetic workloads may not reflect real customer prompts - Mitigation: include top 500 prompt clusters and run A/B comparisons with real traffic samples
Risk: Underestimated ops cost for certain quantized variants - Mitigation: validate with SRE during probe stage and mark as 'requires deeper ops review' if uncertain
Risk: Sales feedback may bias toward revenue-heavy personas - Mitigation: weight interviews by customer cohort and include neutral technical reviewers
Finalize synthetic workload runs with full top-500 prompt set and publish CI job (owner: Priya)
CRO review of heatmap and cost synthesis (owner: Aisha) by 2025-02-20
Integrate decision rubric into scouting workflow and add backfill tasks for dev to create UI flags (owner: Liam)
SRE to validate ops cost estimates for quantized variants and provide final numbers by 2025-02-24
2025-02-10 10:22 - Aisha Patel: Kickoff note. Draft scope sent to Maya + Ethan. Asking SRE for quick cost templates.
2025-02-12 09:40 - Maya Rodriguez: SRE will provide historical onboarding time estimates (weeks) and monitoring delta (on-call cost) by 2025-02-14.
2025-02-14 16:01 - Priya Nair: Uploaded initial synthetic workload results (small sample) to Drive link. Early signal: quantized variant Q1 shows 15% latency improvement but accuracy drop on finserv prompts.
2025-02-15 11:30 - Ethan Park: Sales interviews scheduled (6) for next week. Asked to include 2 mid-market customers as reference checks.
2025-02-16 14:00 - Liam Chen: Draft heatmap v0 ready. Seeking CRO review. Trade-off noted: prioritizing latency-heavy models increases ops cost by estimated 20%.
2025-02-17 09:00 - Aisha Patel: Updated acceptance criteria to require reproducible CI jobs for synthetic workloads. Next: CRO review and finalize telemetry RFC.
This ticket is intentionally scoped to create a repeatable artifact. Avoid deep model onboarding work here; create follow-up ENG tickets for any 'onboard' recommendations. Emphasize conservative cost estimates and clear audit trail for procurement decisions.

title: Adaptive setpoint autoscale soak probe (reverse-sawtooth + steady-state)
summary: Create and validate an adaptive 'setpoint' autoscale soak probe that exercises Dedicated capacity under a compound workload: long steady-state baseline plus periodic reverse-sawtooth bursts that ramp down quickly then sustain mid-level traffic. The harness should measure tokens/sec, p95/p99 generation latency, cold-start incidents, and KV cache hit ratio; detect regressions vs a rolling baseline; and produce signals suitable for integration with our autoscaler fuzzing and alerting pipelines. Unl
text:
Adaptive setpoint autoscale soak probe (reverse-sawtooth + steady-state)

Create and validate an adaptive 'setpoint' autoscale soak probe that exercises Dedicated capacity under a compound workload: long steady-state baseline plus periodic reverse-sawtooth bursts that ramp down quickly then sustain mid-level traffic. The harness should measure tokens/sec, p95/p99 generation latency, cold-start incidents, and KV cache hit ratio; detect regressions vs a rolling baseline; and produce signals suitable for integration with our autoscaler fuzzing and alerting pipelines. Unlike previous burst/soak harnesses, this probe uses a thermostatic controller to nudge target capacity when p95 drifts above configurable thresholds, emulating an operator-adjusted with hysteresis to avoid oscillation.
Harness can replay 24h scenario: 18h steady-state (15k tokens/sec aggregate) + 6x reverse-sawtooth bursts per day
Generates per-minute metrics for tokens/sec, p95, p99, infra CPU/GPU utilization, KV cache hit ratio, and cold-start count
Regression detector flags deviations when p95 exceeds baseline by >15% sustained for 10 minutes
Thermostat controller config reduces noisy autoscale churn: empirically <= 2 scale actions per burst window
Integration hooks emit events to observability pipeline and create a GitHub issue template on regression detection
1) Build workload generator extension to support reverse-sawtooth ramp-down profile and warm/cold request mixes
2) Instrument runtime probes for per-actor KV cache hit/miss and prefix cache retention metrics
3) Implement thermostatic setpoint controller with configurable hysteresis and backoff windows
4) Wire metrics to regression detector; define rolling baseline snapshots with windowed quantiles
5) Run initial 48-hour validation on dedicated staging cluster and iterate thresholds
6) Publish runbook and Grafana dashboard; schedule long soak on customer-like traffic patterns
Thermostat tuning may mask genuine capacity shortages if hysteresis too wide
Reverse-sawtooth bursts could trigger subtle scheduler pathologies not seen in symmetric bursts
KV cache instrumentation adds minor overhead; ensure sampling rate doesn't perturb p95
False positives in regression detector from upstream infra changes (node kernel/hypervisor updates)
2025-11-18 Maya Chen: Created ticket. Motivation: reproduce real-world operator responses to slow p95 drift and validate that autoscaler heuristics won't over-react during short ramp-downs.
2025-11-20 Ethan Park: Spike prototype for reverse-sawtooth generator complete. Initial generator accepts ramp-down duration, trough-time, and steady plateau. Observed that generator needs randomized jitter to avoid synchronized bursts across clients.
2025-11-24 QA Run: 6-hour smoke on staging. Metrics collected. Observed transient p95 spikes during trough recovery — investigating whether KV cache TTL renewal is correlated.
2025-11-26 Feature review with PM/Design: Agreed to expose two knobs in Console: setpoint target (tokens/sec) and hysteresis window (seconds). PM requested a safe default that errs on fewer scale actions.
2025-11-29 Engineering note: Implemented thermostat backoff (exponential) to cap scale frequency. Unit tests added for controller edge cases.
2025-12-01 Observability: Regression detector baseline computed from last 14 days of stable runs. Baseline p95=78ms, baseline tokens/sec=15.2k. Current 24h run shows p95 median=92ms (18% up) during two burst windows — detector flagged and created candidate issue.
2025-12-02 Ethan Park: Investigating flagged regressions. Early hypothesis: node kernel patch on staging resulted in slower CUDA driver response under partial packing. Scheduling dedicated soak on hardware pinned to previous kernel to isolate.
TODO: Run 72h soak with pinned kernel, collect per-shard KV hit ratio, and evaluate thermostat parameters. If pass, plan rollout to canary Dedicated cluster with monitoring playbook.

title: heterogeneous-weights-ingest-runtime-compat-validation
summary: Implement an end-to-end pipeline to ingest heterogeneous open-source model weights (sharded and single-file), convert them deterministically into Redwood canonical runtime artifacts, validate runtime compatibility across supported hardware/quantization paths, and publish a vetted performance profile + pinned version to the model catalog. This work covers manifest schema enforcement, signed provenance checks, deterministic conversion (weights->runtime format), automated quality smoke tests (golde
text:
heterogeneous-weights-ingest-runtime-compat-validation

Implement an end-to-end pipeline to ingest heterogeneous open-source model weights (sharded and single-file), convert them deterministically into Redwood canonical runtime artifacts, validate runtime compatibility across supported hardware/quantization paths, and publish a vetted performance profile + pinned version to the model catalog. This work covers manifest schema enforcement, signed provenance checks, deterministic conversion (weights->runtime format), automated quality smoke tests (golden prompts + token-level diff thresholds), performance microbenchmarks for typical seq lengths, pin recommendation heuristics, and catalog publishing with rollouts and rollback policies.
Weights intake accepts sharded and single-file manifests using signed manifest-v2 format and rejects unsupported layouts with actionable errors
Conversion job produces deterministic runtime artifact (same fingerprint for same input) and stores provenance metadata in artifact storage
Automated smoke suite runs for each converted artifact: generation quality check against golden prompts and token-diff threshold pass/fail
Performance profile (p50/p95 latency, throughput for batch sizes 1/8/32, memory footprint, and quantization compatibility) published to model catalog for each pinned release
Pinning flow recommends a canonical pin string (r-{model}-{arch}-v{semver}) and stores mapping in pin registry; can be consumed by serving router
Canary rollout plan automated: initial 5% traffic, 24h observation window, auto-rollback on latency >x2 baseline or quality regression above configured threshold
1) Ingest: extend artifact-gateway to accept manifest-v2, run signed-manifest verification and store raw blobs
2) Conversion: implement deterministic conversion steps, operator canonicalization, streamed fusion for >64GB peaks
3) Validation: run smoke quality tests, KV-cache hot-start checks, and hardware compatibility matrix runs (A100, H100, T4, CPU-optimized paths)
4) Profiling: automated microbenchmarks for seq lengths 32/128/1024 with batch sizes used by customers; capture p50/p95, tokens/sec, and memory
5) Pin/Publish: run pin-recommender, write stable pin into registry, publish profile to catalog with metadata and fallback mapping
6) Rollout: automated canary with telemetry hooks and automatic rollback triggers; manual gate for enterprise/private deployments
Large shard memory spikes -> mitigation: streamed conversion passes and temporary S3 spill for intermediate tensors
Quality regressions after quantization -> mitigation: include golden prompts and fallback to previous pin or higher-precision quant if threshold breached
Signer/manifest spoofing -> mitigation: require key registry and optional HSM verification for enterprise/private installs
Inconsistent runtime kernels across hardware -> mitigation: publish per-hardware compat matrix and restrict pin to supported runtimes until certified
Publish to staging catalog and run a 48-hour soak with synthetic and recorded customer traffic
Canary: 5% production traffic for 24 hours with automatic rollback rules
Gradual increase to 25% then 100% over 7 days if no regressions
Enterprise/private customers will be offered manual rollout and opt-in to HSM verification
Unit tests for manifest-v2 parsing and signature verification
Determinism fuzz harness for conversion step (repeatability checks across runs)
Smoke generation suite with a curated prompt set (dialog, code, summarization) and token-diff thresholds
Performance benchmark suite across hardware targets and quant modes
End-to-end e2e job run asserting artifact fingerprints and catalog entry exists
Finish conversion streaming PR (weights-converter#497)
Add enterprise HSM verification toggle and doc the opt-in flow
Finalize quality regression detection thresholds with PM and Research
Schedule demo with Solutions Eng for initial enterprise pilot on 2026-03-20
2026-03-02: Kickoff with runtime and ingest teams. Agreed manifest-v2 as baseline for signed provenance; schema PR opened in infra-schemas repo.
2026-03-04: Decided on deterministic conversion layer using fixed RNG seeds, layer reordering normalization, and float->quant fallback semantics documented in conversion spec doc.
2026-03-06: Spike results: conversion time for 30B sharded model ~ 24min on c6g-converter-worker; memory peak observed at 110GB during fusion step — need to split fusion into streamed passes for very large shards.
2026-03-08: Added KV-cache compatibility test to smoke suite to ensure runtime hot-start works for chat workloads.
2026-03-10: UX/PM review: catalog page must expose per-pin performance histograms, determinism checksum, and fallback targets (smaller quantized or CPU fallback) — mockups queued (DES-421).
2026-03-12: First end-to-end run succeeded for community-model 'bardok-3b' -> artifacts persisted, profile published to staging catalog. Quality delta within thresholds.
Decision: Default pin format will include architecture and quantization suffix (example: r-bardok-3b-archA-v1.0-q4f) to make routing unambiguous.
Open: finalize thresholds for token-diff-based quality regression detection (suggested: BLEU-like token overlap + top-k next-token probability gap).
@Aisha Patel (PM): Need to ensure audit logs include original source URL and signer identity for compliance reviews.
@Luca Moretti (Security): Recommend HSM-backed manifest verification in private deployments; scope out as a separate opt-in module.
@Sasha Ivanov (Runtime): Observed a non-deterministic op order in fused attention kernel; adding a canonical operator reorder pass in converter.
@Design: small tweak to catalog UI to surface 'regression risk' badge when a model's quantized profile shows >5% quality delta vs fp32 baseline.
